content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch against 26edf3340c5c84297ede274973eeba3b7085ad30, excluding live .ralph-burning state. Found three correctness issues. Verification run: nix build, cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --locked -- -D warnings, and cargo test --locked --features test-stub all completed successfully.

## Amendments

1. src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:8217 now treats `classified_findings` as authoritative and returns at 8235-8237 whenever any classified item exists, so legacy `follow_up_or_amendments` are dropped instead of merged. Combined with the skip path at 8288-8294, a review payload containing one deferred classified finding plus an ordinary fix-now follow-up can skip `apply_fixes` and advance without remediation. This changes the `fix_current_bead` amendment flow; keep merging non-duplicate legacy follow-ups as fix-current items while filtering only non-fix classifications out of the immediate queue.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: Can silently drop required current-bead fixes from mixed or partially migrated review payloads.
2. src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:484-490 treats a failed `br update --status=closed` as success if a follow-up read sees the bead closed, but src/adapters/br_process.rs:1335-1345 clears the pending mutation record on that failed update. In that false-failure case, `sync_after_close` has no durable pending record to flush, so the run can record success while the local bead close is never pushed. Restore a pending `update_bead_status(..., "closed")` record before accepting the observed closed state; mirror the same recovery in the CLI terminal close path at src/cli/run.rs:1220-1229.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: The codebase already handles false-failed `br update` for claims; the new close path needs the same crash-safe sync guarantee.
3. src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1278-1283 accepts a `covered_by_existing_bead` target without checking whether it is the current `bead_id`, and 1320-1327 then posts the reconciliation comment to that target. If a reviewer accidentally points `covered_by_bead_id` or legacy `mapped_to_bead_id` at the active bead, this path modifies the current bead even though the contract says covered-by-existing must not modify it. Add a warn-and-skip guard when the normalized target matches the current bead.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: Prevents invalid review metadata from violating the no-current-bead-modification acceptance rule.
